bitty snippets tutorials examples documentation
  • bitty tag attributes [0005-bitty-connection]
    • data-connect [data-connect]
      Description
      • Each <bitty-8-0> requires a data-connect attribute.
      • The attribute defines the path to the JavaScript module to load.
      • Modules must contain an export const bitty = {} in order to work with bitty.
      • Multiple <bitty-8-0> tags can be placed on a single page.
      • Multiple modules can contain functions with the same name. Each will respond to incoming signals regardless of their source. (namespacing is on the roadmap to provide a seperation of concerns.)
      Examples
      • Make a connection to a module. [0010-basic-connection]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0010-basic-connection/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_B1D0F_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B1D0F_alfa">Send B1D0F_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_B1D0F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tag for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0010-basic-connection/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B1D0F_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B1D0F_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Load multiple bitty modules on the same page. [0020-multiple-instances]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0020-multiple-instances/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0020-multiple-instances/test_javascript_2.js"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_A2EB2_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A2EB2_alfa">Send A2EB2_alfa</button>

<div data-r="signal_A2EB2_bravo">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A2EB2_bravo">Send A2EB2_bravo</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A2EB2_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A2EB2_bravo(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tags for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0020-multiple-instances/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A2EB2_alfa]").click();
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A2EB2_bravo]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;

  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A2EB2_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });

  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A2EB2_bravo]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • All modules with a signal function name respond to it. [0030-same-name]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0030-same-name/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0030-same-name/test_javascript_2.js"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_9E5B8_bravo" class="example">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_9E5B8_charlie" class="example">waiting</div>

<button data-s="signal_9E5B8_alfa">Send 9E5B8_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_9E5B8_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  /************************************************
   * This function receives the initial
   * `signal_9E5B8_alfa` then triggers `signal_9E5B8_bravo`
   * which is unique to this module.
   ************************************************/
  bitty.trigger("signal_9E5B8_bravo");
}

export function signal_9E5B8_bravo(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_9E5B8_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  /************************************************
   * This function receives the initial
   * `signal_9E5B8_alfa` then triggers `signal_9E5B8_charlie`
   * which is unique to this module.
   ************************************************/
  bitty.trigger("signal_9E5B8_charlie");
}

export function signal_9E5B8_charlie(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        Notes
        • [x] Each of these modules contains the same function named signal_9E5B8_alfa.
        • [x] When that signal is received, each one triggers another signal to an independent function (signal_9E5B8_bravo in the first module and signal_9E5B8_charlie in the second).
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tags for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-connect/0030-same-name/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_9E5B8_alfa]").click();
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;

  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9E5B8_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });

  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9E5B8_charlie]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • data-run [data-run]
      Description
      • data-run is an optional attribute that can be used on a bitty-8-0 tag.
      • If a data-run attribute exists it fires the signals from its value.
      • Multiple signals can be triggered from the data-run attribute.
      Examples
      • Run a function when the module has loaded. [0010-init-a-function]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0010-init-a-function/test_javascript.js" 
  data-run="signal_E34C9_alfa"></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_E34C9_alfa">waiting</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_E34C9_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tag for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0010-init-a-function/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E34C9_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Fire multiple signals with `data-run`. [0020-init-multiple]
        HTML
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0020-init-multiple/test_javascript.js"
  data-run="signal_7B23F_alfa signal_7B23F_bravo"
></bitty-8-0>

<div data-r="signal_7B23F_alfa" class="example">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_7B23F_bravo" class="example">waiting</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_7B23F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

export function signal_7B23F_bravo(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <!-- the bitty tag for this example is embedded directly in the example HTML -->
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0005-bitty-connection/data-run/0020-init-multiple/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7B23F_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7B23F_bravo]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
  • html attributes [0010-data-attributes]
    • data-listeners [data-listeners]
      Description
      • bitty listens for click and input events on data-s elements by default.
      • The data-listeners attribute can be used to change the events that an element listens for.
      • If a data-listeners attribute is used the element stops listening for the default click and input events.
      • The default click and input events can be added back in as part of the data-listeners to continue listening for them.
      Examples
      • Listen for a different type of event. [0010-custom-signal]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_6E38F_alfa" class="example">waiting</div>
<div 
  data-listeners="keydown" 
  data-s="signal_6E38F_alfa" 
  contenteditable="true"
  class="style_signal_6E38F_alfa"
></div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_6E38F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        Notes
        • This example uses a keydown listener to allow automated testing (e.g. compared to a mouseover which is harder to automate)
        • bitty provides a mapKey() function that's likely to be of more use if you need to monitor key presses. Details on it are below.
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0010-custom-signal/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0010-custom-signal/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

<style>
.style_signal_6E38F_alfa {
  background-color: var(--faded-info-color);
  border-radius: var(--default-border-radius);
  border: var(--accent-border);
  height: 3rem;
  padding: var(--default-padding);
  width: 10rem;
}
</style>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);

  const event_signal_6E38F_alfa = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "a",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_6E38F_alfa]").dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F_alfa);

  // add to innerHTML as well for the visual update.
  // It doesn't affect the test. It helps avoid
  // confusion that would occur if there was
  // nothing in the div after the test was run.
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_6E38F_alfa]").innerHTML = "a";

  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_6E38F_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The default event types (i.e. `click` and `input`) don't trigger if there's a `data-listeners` attribute exists on a `data-s` element and the value does not contain the default event types. [0020-defaults-removed]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_35510_alfa">won't change on click</div>
<button data-listeners="keydown" data-s="signal_35510_alfa">Click does nothing for 35510_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_35510_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  // el.dataset.solo = true;
  el.innerHTML = "todo";
  // el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        won't change on click
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0020-defaults-removed/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0020-defaults-removed/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_35510_alfa]").click();
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /won't change on click/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_35510_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The default `click` and `input` event types can be added back into `data-listeners` to continue listening for them. [0030-defaults-added]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_7B421_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-listeners="keydown click" data-s="signal_7B421_alfa">Send 7B421_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_7B421_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0030-defaults-added/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-listeners/0030-defaults-added/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_7B421_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7B421_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • data-r [data-r]
      Description
      Examples
      • Receive a single signal. [0010-basic-receive]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_E9FCA_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_E9FCA_alfa">Send E9FCA_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_E9FCA_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-r/0010-basic-receive/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-r/0010-basic-receive/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_E9FCA_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E9FCA_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • data-s [data-s]
      Description
      • The data-s attribute defines what signals an element sends when an event it's associated with fires.
      • Multiple signals can be sent at the same time.
      • Signals sent that have no corresponding receivers trigger their related function once with a null value for the third/element argument.
      Examples
      • Send one signal to a single receiver. [0010-single-signal]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B28CE_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B28CE_alfa">Send B28CE_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_B28CE_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0010-single-signal/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0010-single-signal/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B28CE_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B28CE_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_294BF_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_294BF_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Send multiple signals to multiple receivers. [0020-multiple-signals]

<div data-r="signal_91921_bravo" class="example">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_91921_alfa signal_91921_bravo">
  Send 91921_alfa
</button>

        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0020-multiple-signals/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0010-data-attributes/data-s/0020-multiple-signals/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_91921_alfa").click();
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_91921_bravo").click();
  bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_91921_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_91921_bravo]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
  • bitty functions [0030-bitty-functions]
    • addListener(event, signals) [addListener]
      Description
      • [x] Adds an event listener for a given event and sets it up to send the corresponding signals when it occurs.
      • [x] Can be used to fire signals for events that aren't associated with an element.
      • [x] Can be used to fire signals that could be associated with an element (e.g. you can add a click listener that listens for any click on the page)
      • [x] Signal functions are called with the three arguments. If there's no receiver, it's signalName(event, null, element). If there's a receiver, it's signalName(event, null, element)
      Examples
      • Connect events to signals. [0010-set-up-listner]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_376FE_alfa">waiting</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function initialize_signal_376FE_alfa() {
  bitty.addListener("event_signal_376FE_alfa", "signal_376FE_alfa");
}

export function signal_376FE_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        Notes
        • This test uses a custom event to automate the demo.
        • The featured uses addEventListener() and can use any event that works with the method (e.g. resize events on the window).
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/addListener/0010-set-up-listner/test_javascript.js"
  data-run="initialize_signal_376FE_alfa"
></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/addListener/0010-set-up-listner/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  const testEvent = new TestEvent_signal_376FE_alfa();
  dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  await bitty.sleep(100);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_376FE_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}

class TestEvent_signal_376FE_alfa extends Event {
  constructor(signals) {
    super("event_signal_376FE_alfa", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
    • copy(selector) [copy]
      Description
      • [x] Copies the text from the given selector via document.querySelector().
      • [x] The copied text comes from a .value if the element has one.
      • [x] Otherwise, the text copied text comes from .innerHTML.
      • [x] Returns true if the copy succeeded.
      • [x] Returns false if the copy failed.
      Examples
      • Copy text from a `.value` [0010-copy-from-value]
        HTML
        <input type="text" id="text_signal_D0CD6_alfa" value="Test .value content for signal_D0CD6_alfa" />

<p>
  This example uses the OS copy/paste board. It can't
  be properly tested from inside the browser. 
  Use this button to copy the text from above
  and paste it into another location 
  to watch it in action.
</p>

<button data-s="signal_D0CD6_alfa">Copy Text D0CD6_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_D0CD6_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.copy("#text_signal_D0CD6_alfa");
}
        Result

        This example uses the OS copy/paste board. It can't be properly tested from inside the browser. Use this button to copy the text from above and paste it into another location to watch it in action.

        bitty tag for the test verification

  Testing this example is done manually

    • fetch(url, options = {}); [fetch]
      Description
      • [x] Fetches a JSON from a URL.
      • [x] Returns the JSON object if the fetch succeeds and the response parses as JSON.
      • [x] Returns undefined if either the fetch fails or the JSON fails to parse.
      • [x] Accepts and optional options object which is passed to the fetch call (e.g. for headers).
      • [x] Logs any errors to the console.
      Examples
      • Fetch and return a JSON object. [0010-basic-fetch]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_BAA6F_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_BAA6F_alfa">Send BAA6F_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_BAA6F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const json = await bitty.fetch("/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-json.json");
  if (json.alfa === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetch/0010-basic-fetch/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetch/0010-basic-fetch/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_BAA6F_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_BAA6F_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return `undefined` if the fetch fails. [0020-fetch-fail-is-error]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_79EC4_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_79EC4_alfa">Send 79EC4_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_79EC4_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  console.error(
    "NOTE: the 404 missing error for intentionally-missing-file.json is an expected error from testing.",
  );
  const response = await bitty.fetch(
    "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/intentionally-missing-file.json",
  );
  if (response === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

        waiting

export async function runTestWithErrors() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_79EC4_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_79EC4_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return `undefined` if JSON fails to parse. [0030-parse-fail-is-error]

      • Pass options to the fetch call. [0040-pass-options]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_DB9CF_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_DB9CF_alfa">Send DB9CF_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_DB9CF_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const options = {
    method: "GET",
  };
  const json = await bitty.fetch("/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-json.json", options);
  if (json.alfa === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_DB9CF_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_DB9CF_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • fetchHTML(url, options = {}) [fetchHTML]
      Description
      • [x] Fetches a URL to get templates from.
      • [x] Each <template> tag at the root of the file is converted into a document fragment.
      • [x] The templates are returned as an objects.
      • [x] The id attribute on each <template> is used as the key for the template's document fragment in the object.
      • [x] <template> elements without an id are ignored.
      • [x] Options can be passed to the fetch call with the optional section argument.
      Examples
      • fetchTemplates(url, options) [0010-fetch-html]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_71C04_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_71C04_alfa">Send 71C04_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_71C04_alfa(_, __, el) {
  const templates = await bitty.fetchHTML(
    "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-templates/index.html",
  );
  const checkEl = templates.alfa.querySelector("div");
  if (checkEl.innerHTML = "alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchHTML/0010-fetch-html/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/fetchHTML/0010-fetch-html/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_71C04_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_71C04_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      Notes
      • The source template file used is /versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/valid-templates/index.html
      • You'll have to view source on the page to see anything since the templates don't render by default.
    • fetchSVG(url, options = {}) [fetchSVG]
      Description
      • [x] Fetches one or more SVGs from a URL.
      • [x] The file is parsed as HTML with the expectation that it will contain only <svg> tags (i.e. there is no <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, or related tags).
      • [x] Each <svg> tag that has an id attribute is included in the returned object with the id acting as the key.
      Examples
      • Fetch SVGs from a URL. [0010-fetch-svgs]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_735BF_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_735BF_alfa">Send 735BF_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_735BF_alfa(_, __, el) {
  const svgs = await bitty.fetchSVG(
    "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/samples/multiple-svgs/index.html",
  );
  if (svgs["example_svg_alfa"].querySelector("text").innerHTML === "alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_735BF_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_735BF_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • load(key, fallback = null) [load]
      Description
      • [x] Loads JSON data from localStorage if it exists and returns it.
      • [x] Provides for an optional object fallback to use if there's nothing in storage with the given key.
      • [x] Returns undefined if the localStorage doesn't exist and there's no fallback.
      • [x] Returns undefined if the value in localStorage can't be parsed as JSON.
      Examples
      • Load existing JSON [0010-load-existing-json]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_FBA10_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_FBA10_alfa">Send FBA10_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_FBA10_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const json = bitty.load("FBA10_alfa");
  if (json.alfa === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/load/0010-load-existing-json/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/load/0010-load-existing-json/test_verification_javascript.js"
  data-run="loadSampleDatasignal_FBA10_alfa"
  ></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function loadSampleDatasignal_FBA10_alfa() {
  bitty.save("FBA10_alfa", { alfa: "bravo" });
}

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_FBA10_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_FBA10_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an optional object fallback if there is nothing in storage for the given key. [0020-object-fallback]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_D2343_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_D2343_alfa">Send D2343_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_D2343_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const json = bitty.load("no-storage-for-signal_D2343_alfa", {
    targetsignal_D2343_alfa: true,
  });
  if (json.targetsignal_D2343_alfa === true) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_D2343_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_D2343_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return `undefined` if there's not a value in storage for the key and there's no fallback. [0030-undefined-if-no-storage-or-fallback]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_D6B1F_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_D6B1F_alfa">Send D6B1F_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_D6B1F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const result = bitty.load("intentionally-missing-signal_D6B1F_alfa");
  if (result === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_D6B1F_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_D6B1F_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Return undefined if there's a value in localStorage for the key but it doesn't parse as JSON. [0040-undefined-if-can-not-parse]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_616DA_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_616DA_alfa">Send 616DA_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_616DA_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const result = bitty.load("invalid_signal_616DA_alfa");
  if (result === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/load/0040-undefined-if-can-not-parse/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/load/0040-undefined-if-can-not-parse/test_verification_javascript.js"
  data-run="loadSampleDatasignal_616DA_alfa"
></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function loadSampleDatasignal_616DA_alfa() {
  localStorage.setItem("invalid_signal_616DA_alfa", "not a valid json string");
}

export async function runTestWithErrors() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_616DA_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_616DA_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • mapKey(key, signals, modKeys = [], options = {}) [mapKey]
      Description
      • [x] Maps a key event to a signal signal.
      • [x] Maps a key event to multiple signals.
      • [x] Uses keydown by default.
      • [x] Listener can be set to keyup.
      • [x] Modifier keys can be defined (see modKeyAliases() for the list of keys).
      • [x] The event won't trigger if unknown modifier keys are specified.
      • [x] Prevents the browser from responding to the key press via preventDefault.
      • [x] preventDefault can be turned off.
      • [x] Key Codes can be used instead of strings by using a number instead of the character.
      Examples
      • Map a single key to a single signal [0010-map-single-to-single]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_07868_alfa">waiting</div>
<div>Press `q`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_07868_alfa() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("q", "signal_07868_alfa");
}

export function signal_07868_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        Press `q`
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 
data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/mapKey/0010-map-single-to-single/test_javascript.js"
data-run="setKey_signal_07868_alfa"
></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/mapKey/0010-map-single-to-single/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "q",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_07868_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Map a single key to multiple signals. [0020-map-single-to-multiple]

}

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "w",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_CFB66_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Map a key with modifier keys (e.g. "shift", "alt", etc...) [0030-mod-keys]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_DE6CE_alfa">waiting</div>
<div>Press `ctrl + e`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("e", "signal_DE6CE_alfa", ["ctrlKey"]);
}

  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "e",
    ctrlKey: true,
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_DE6CE_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Aliases (e.g. `ALT`) can be used in place of the explicit JavaScript event names (e.g. `altKey`). The full list of options is available in `modKeyAliases()`. [0035-mod-aliases]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_6B685_alfa">waiting</div>
<div>Press `ctrl + r`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("r", "signal_6B685_alfa", ["CTRL"]);
}

export function signal_6B685_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "r",
    ctrlKey: true,
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_6B685_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Unknown modifier keys are not allowed. [0038-unknow-mods-fail]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_273EF_alfa" class="manual-test">there's no output from this example.</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_273EF_alfa() {
  /******************************************
   * This function is called when the
   * test is run.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("t", "signal_273EF_alfa", ["invalidModKey"]);
}

export function signal_273EF_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

        there's no output from this example.

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTestWithErrors() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  bitty.trigger("setKey_signal_273EF_alfa");
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "t",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /there's no output from this example./;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_273EF_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Allow default action to occur (i.e. don't apply `preventDefault()` to the event). [0050-allow-default]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_9EAED_alfa">waiting</div>
<div>Press `/`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_9EAED_alfa() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("/", "signal_9EAED_alfa", [], { preventDefault: false });
}

export function signal_9EAED_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    key: "/",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9EAED_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use `keyup` instead of `keydown` [0060-keyup]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_78314_alfa">waiting</div>
<div>Press `b`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_78314_alfa() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey("b", "signal_78314_alfa", [], { listener: "keyup" });
}

export function signal_78314_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

        waiting
        Press `b`
        view test harness

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keyup", {
    key: "b",
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_78314_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use a key code instead number of a character string. [0080-key-codes]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_80715_alfa">waiting</div>
<div>Press `y`</div>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function setKey_signal_80715_alfa() {
  /******************************************
   * This functions is run via the `data-run`
   * attribute in the <bitty-8-0> tag.
   ******************************************/
  bitty.mapKey(89, "signal_80715_alfa");
}

export function signal_80715_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        Press `y`
        view test harness

        <bitty-8-0 
data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/mapKey/0080-key-codes/test_javascript.js"
data-run="setKey_signal_80715_alfa"
></bitty-8-0>

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const event_signal_6E38F = new KeyboardEvent("keydown", {
    keyCode: 89,
    bubbles: true,
    cancelable: true,
  });
  document.dispatchEvent(event_signal_6E38F);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_80715_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      Notes
      • preventDefault() is applied unless otherwise specified.
      • If preventDefault() occurs for any key via mapKey() it affects all the other uses of that key (i.e. trying to use preventDefault() for a key in one place and not in another causes a race condition with undefined behaviour).
    • modKeyAlises() [modKeyAliases]
      Description
      • [x] Returns the object that holds the available aliases for modifier keys used with mapKey()
      • [x] The values sent to mapKey() are case insensitive (they always transformed to lowercase when doing the mapping).
      Examples
      • Get the aliases [0010-get-aliases]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_13C5D_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_13C5D_alfa">Send 13C5D_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_13C5D_alfa(_, __, el) {
  if (bitty.modKeyAliases().alt === "altKey") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/modKeyAliases/0010-get-aliases/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/modKeyAliases/0010-get-aliases/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_13C5D_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_13C5D_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • qs(selector, el = null) [qs]
      Description
      • [x] Alias for document.querySelector(selector) if no second argument is passed.
      • [x] Alias for el.querySelector(selector) if an element is passed as the second argument.
      Examples
      • Get an element from the document. [0010-get-element]
        HTML
        <div id="target_signal_B7BA2_alfa"></div>

<div data-r="signal_B7BA2_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B7BA2_alfa">Send B7BA2_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_B7BA2_alfa(_, __, el) {
  const fromDefault = document.querySelector("#target_signal_B7BA2_alfa");
  const fromBitty = bitty.qs("#target_signal_B7BA2_alfa");
  if (fromBitty.isSameNode(fromDefault)) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qs/0010-get-element/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qs/0010-get-element/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B7BA2_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B7BA2_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Get an element from another element. [0020-from-element]
        HTML
        <div id="target_alfa_signal_A9BED_alfa">
  <div id="target_bravo_signal_A9BED_alfa"></div>
</div>

<div data-r="signal_A9BED_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A9BED_alfa">Send A9BED_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A9BED_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const fromNative = document.querySelector("#target_bravo_signal_A9BED_alfa");
  const alfa = bitty.qs("#target_alfa_signal_A9BED_alfa");
  const bravo = bitty.qs("#target_bravo_signal_A9BED_alfa", alfa);
  if (bravo.isSameNode(fromNative)) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qs/0020-from-element/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qs/0020-from-element/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A9BED_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A9BED_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • qsa(selector, el = null) [qsa]
      Description
      • [] Alias for document.querySelectorAll(selector)
      Examples
      • Get a group of elements from the document. [0010-get-elements]
        HTML
        <div class="target_signal_00C0F_alfa">alfa signal_00C0F_alfa</div>
<div class="target_signal_00C0F_alfa">bravo signal_00C0F_alfa</div>
<div class="target_signal_00C0F_alfa">charlie signal_00C0F_alfa</div>

<div data-r="signal_00C0F_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_00C0F_alfa">Send 00C0F_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_00C0F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const els = bitty.qsa(".target_signal_00C0F_alfa");
  if (els[2].innerHTML === "charlie signal_00C0F_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        alfa signal_00C0F_alfa
        bravo signal_00C0F_alfa
        charlie signal_00C0F_alfa
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qsa/0010-get-elements/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qsa/0010-get-elements/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_00C0F_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_00C0F_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Get a group of elements from another element. [0020-from-element]
        HTML
        <div id="container_signal_C0EE2_alfa">
  <div class="target_signal_C0EE2_alfa">alfa signal_C0EE2_alfa</div>
  <div class="target_signal_C0EE2_alfa">bravo signal_C0EE2_alfa</div>
  <div class="target_signal_C0EE2_alfa">charlie signal_C0EE2_alfa</div>
</div>

<div data-r="signal_C0EE2_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_C0EE2_alfa">Send C0EE2_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_C0EE2_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const container = bitty.qs("#container_signal_C0EE2_alfa");
  const els = bitty.qsa(".target_signal_C0EE2_alfa");
  if (els[2].innerHTML === "charlie signal_C0EE2_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        alfa signal_C0EE2_alfa
        bravo signal_C0EE2_alfa
        charlie signal_C0EE2_alfa
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qsa/0020-from-element/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/qsa/0020-from-element/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_C0EE2_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_C0EE2_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • render(source, substitutions = {}) [render]
      Description
      • [x] Used to render a template with substitutions.
      • [x] Takes input from a Document Fragment.
      • [x] Takes input from an HTML Element.
      • [x] Takes input from a string.
      • [x] Always returns a Document Fragment.
      • [x] Applies substitutions from an optional object passed as the second argument.
      • [x] Substitution is done by converting the input into a string and doing find/replace on that string.
      • [x] Keys of the substitution object are the strings that are searched for.
      • [x] Values from the substitution object are what's used to replace the found strings.
      • [x] Replacements can be a string.
      • [x] Replacements can can be an array of strings which get joined directly together.
      • [x] Replacements can be an HTML Element.
      • [x] Replacements can be an array of HTML Elements.
      • [x] Replacements can be a Document Fragment.
      • [x] Replacements can be an array of Document Fragments.
      Examples
      • Use a document fragment for input. [0010-render-without-subs]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_E74C7_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_E74C7_alfa">Send E74C7_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "signal_E74C7_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

export function signal_E74C7_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.innerHTML === "signal_E74C7_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0010-render-without-subs/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0010-render-without-subs/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_E74C7_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_E74C7_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use a string for input. [0020-input-string]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_736E9_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_736E9_alfa">Send 736E9_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_736E9_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const html = bitty.render("<div>signal_736E9_alfa</div");
  const result = html.firstChild.innerHTML;
  if (result === "signal_736E9_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example

  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_736E9_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_736E9_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an HTML Element as input. [0025-element-input]

  const html = bitty.render(input);
  const result = html.firstChild.innerHTML;

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_4A624_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_4A624_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Perform string substitutions. [0040-string-subs]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B1061_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B1061_alfa">Send B1061_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_B1061_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_B1061_alfa": "UPDATED_signal_B1061_alfa",
};

export function signal_B1061_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_B1061_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0040-string-subs/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0040-string-subs/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B1061_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B1061_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an array of strings for replacements. [0045-string-array]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_1AC47_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_1AC47_alfa">Send 1AC47_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_1AC47_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_1AC47_alfa": ["UPDATED_", "signal_1AC47_alfa"],
};

export function signal_1AC47_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_1AC47_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_1AC47_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_1AC47_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an HTML Element for the replacement. [0050-element-sub]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_F3DE4_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_F3DE4_alfa">Send F3DE4_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_F3DE4_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subEl = document.createElement("div");
subEl.innerHTML = "UPDATED_signal_F3DE4_alfa";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_F3DE4_alfa": subEl,
};

export function signal_F3DE4_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const checkEl = output.firstChild;
  if (checkEl.firstChild.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_F3DE4_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_F3DE4_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_F3DE4_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an array of HTML Elements as the replacement payload. [0055-element-array]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_02C95_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_02C95_alfa">Send 02C95_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_02C95_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subEl1 = document.createElement("div");
subEl1.innerHTML = "UPDATED";
const subEl2 = document.createElement("div");
subEl2.innerHTML = "signal_02C95_alfa";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_02C95_alfa": [subEl1, subEl2],
};

export function signal_02C95_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  if (output.firstChild.children[1].innerHTML === "signal_02C95_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0055-element-array/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
