        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_02C95_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_02C95_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use a Document Fragment for a replacement. [0060-frag-sub]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_1EDC7_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_1EDC7_alfa">Send 1EDC7_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_1EDC7_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subFragment = document.createElement("template");
subFragment.innerHTML = "<div>UPDATED_signal_1EDC7_alfa</div>";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_1EDC7_alfa": subFragment.content,
};

export function signal_1EDC7_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  if (output.firstChild.firstChild.innerHTML === "UPDATED_signal_1EDC7_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0060-frag-sub/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0060-frag-sub/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_1EDC7_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_1EDC7_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Use an array of document fragments for as a replacement value. [0065-frag-array]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B15F2_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B15F2_alfa">Send B15F2_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

const input = document.createDocumentFragment();
const newEl = document.createElement("div");
newEl.innerHTML = "TARGET_signal_B15F2_alfa";
input.appendChild(newEl);

const subFragment1 = document.createElement("template");
subFragment1.innerHTML = "<div>UPDATED</div>";

const subFragment2 = document.createElement("template");
subFragment2.innerHTML = "<div>signal_B15F2_alfa</div>";

const subs = {
  "TARGET_signal_B15F2_alfa": [subFragment1.content, subFragment2.content],
};

export function signal_B15F2_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const output = bitty.render(input, subs);
  const result = output.firstChild.children[1].innerHTML;
  if (result === "signal_B15F2_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0065-frag-array/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/render/0065-frag-array/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B15F2_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B15F2_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • save(key, data) [save]
      Description
      Examples
      • saveJSON(key, data) [0010-save-json]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_799B4_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_799B4_alfa">Send 799B4_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_799B4_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const result = bitty.save("799B4_alfa", {});
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/save/0010-save-json/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/save/0010-save-json/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_799B4_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_799B4_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • send(payload, signal) [send]
      Description
      • [x] Sends a payload to a single from inside another function.
      • [x] One more signals can be sent at the same time.
      • [x] Signals are sent as events that travel up the DOM. They can be received and run by any bitty related function in any order (i.e. the signals might execute in an order different to the initial list).
      • [x] The first argument passed to a signal function is the payload from the .send(payload, signals) call.
      • [x] The second argument is always null.
      • [x] If there are receiving elements, then the function is called once for each with the element passed in for the third argument.
      • [x] If there are no receiving elements, then the function is called one time with a null value for the third argument.
      Examples
      • Used `.send()` to pass a payload to a signal function. [0010-basic-send]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_8919E_bravo">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_8919E_alfa">Send 8919E_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_8919E_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.send({ key_signal_8919E_alfa: "value_signal_8919E_alfa" }, "signal_8919E_bravo");
}

export function signal_8919E_bravo(payload, __, el) {
  if (payload.key_signal_8919E_alfa === "value_signal_8919E_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/send/0010-basic-send/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/send/0010-basic-send/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_8919E_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_8919E_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Send the same payload to multiple signals. [0020-multiple-sends]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_A8E3D_bravo">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_A8E3D_charlie">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A8E3D_alfa">Send A8E3D_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A8E3D_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.send({ key_signal_A8E3D_alfa: "value_signal_A8E3D_alfa" }, "signal_A8E3D_bravo signal_A8E3D_charlie");
}

export function signal_A8E3D_bravo(payload, __, el) {
  if (payload.key_signal_A8E3D_alfa === "value_signal_A8E3D_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

export function signal_A8E3D_charlie(payload, __, el) {
  if (payload.key_signal_A8E3D_alfa === "value_signal_A8E3D_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/send/0020-multiple-sends/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/send/0020-multiple-sends/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A8E3D_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A8E3D_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
  const checkEls3 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A8E3D_charlie]");
  checkEls3.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The second argument passed to a signal function from `.send()` is always `null`. [0030-arg2-is-null]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_9D3AE_bravo">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_9D3AE_alfa">Send 9D3AE_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_9D3AE_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.send({}, "signal_9D3AE_bravo");
}

export function signal_9D3AE_bravo(_, sender, el) {
  if (sender === null) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/send/0030-arg2-is-null/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/send/0030-arg2-is-null/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_9D3AE_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9D3AE_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • If there are no receiving elements that correspond to a `.send()` call the function is passed a `null` for the third argument and fires exactly one time. [0040-arg3-null-if-no-receiver]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_03E2A_charlie">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_03E2A_alfa">Send 03E2A_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_03E2A_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.send({}, "signal_03E2A_bravo");
}

export function signal_03E2A_bravo(_, __, el) {
  if (el === null) {
    bitty.send({}, "signal_03E2A_charlie");
  }
}

export function signal_03E2A_charlie(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_03E2A_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_03E2A_charlie]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • setCSS(key, value) [setCSS]
      Description
      • Sets a custom CSS property variable to the given value.
      Examples
      • Set a custom CSS property variable. [0010-set-prop]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_7D5A1_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_7D5A1_alfa">Send 7D5A1_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_7D5A1_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  bitty.setCSS("--test_signal_7D5A1_alfa", "crimson");
  if (
    getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue(
      "--test_signal_7D5A1_alfa",
    ) === "crimson"
  ) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/setCSS/0010-set-prop/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/setCSS/0010-set-prop/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_7D5A1_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_7D5A1_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • sleep(ms) [sleep]
      Description
      • Provides an async sleep for the given number of milliseconds.
      Examples
      • Sleep for N milliseconds. [0010-do-sleep]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B5E30_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B5E30_alfa">Send B5E30_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function signal_B5E30_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const t1 = performance.now();
  await bitty.sleep(100);
  const t2 = performance.now();
  if (t2 - t1 > 80) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/sleep/0010-do-sleep/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/sleep/0010-do-sleep/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B5E30_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B5E30_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • sort [sort]
      Description
      • [x] A function to be used with Array.prototype.sort() that does a case insensitive sort on strings.
      • [x] The method's signature is .sort(a, b) but is called without the params when used in an array .sort() call (i.e. items.sort(bitty.sort)).
      Examples
      • Do a case insensitive sort of an array (updating the array itself) [0010-do-sort]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_FBF54_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_FBF54_alfa">Send FBF54_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_FBF54_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const items = [
    "bravo",
    "Alfa",
    "Delta",
    "charlie",
  ];
  items.sort(bitty.sort);
  if (items[1] === "bravo") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/sort/0010-do-sort/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/sort/0010-do-sort/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_FBF54_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_FBF54_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • tee(input, log = true) [tee]
      Description
      • The bitty.tee() method is modeled after the tee command line tool.
      • Input sent to see is returned without modification.
      • By default, the input is also sent to content.log.
      • The log output can be turned off by setting the second argument to false or 0.
      Examples
      • Return the input passed to tee while also logging it out to the console. [0010-demo-tee]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_78164_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_78164_alfa">Send 78164_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_78164_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.tee(bitty.time());
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/tee/0010-demo-tee/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/tee/0010-demo-tee/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_78164_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_78164_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • time(date = new Date()) [time]
      Description
      • Outputs a timestamp in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS for the current locale.
      • The hour in HH is based of a 24 hour clock (e.g. 3PM is 15).
      • If a Date is passed to the function the output is based off it.
      • If no argument is provided the function uses new Date() for the time.
      Examples
      • Display the current time. [0010-current-time]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_010EE_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_010EE_alfa">Send 010EE_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_010EE_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const timestamp = bitty.time();
  el.innerHTML = timestamp;
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/time/0010-current-time/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/time/0010-current-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_010EE_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_010EE_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Display a specific time. [0020-specific-time]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_05431_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_05431_alfa">Send 05431_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_05431_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const date = new Date(2026, 0, 1, 13, 45, 55);
  const timestamp = bitty.time(date);
  el.innerHTML = timestamp;
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/time/0020-specific-time/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/time/0020-specific-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_05431_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_05431_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      Notes
      • The purpose of this function is to provide a concise display of a time for the current locale. It is not a valid ISO 8601, RFC 3339, or RFC 9557 timestamp.
    • timeMs(date = new Date()) [timeMs]
      Description
      • Operates the same as time() but adds milliseconds to the end of the string.
      Examples
      • Display the current time with milliseconds. [0010-current-time]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_5415A_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_5415A_alfa">Send 5415A_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_5415A_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const timestamp = bitty.timeMs();
  el.innerHTML = timestamp;
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/timeMs/0010-current-time/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/timeMs/0010-current-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_5415A_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_5415A_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/timeMs/0020-specific-time/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_85842_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_85842_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • trigger(signals) [trigger]
      Description
      • [x] Used to trigger a signal from inside another function.
      • [x] Can trigger one single or multiple.
      • [x] If a signal is triggered and there are corresponding receiver elements (i.e. they have a matching data-r attribute), each one is processed individually (the process is the same as what happens with data-s).
      • [x] The first argument passed to a triggered signal is a custom event with the type bittytrigger.
      • [x] The internal details of the bittytrigger event type is an implementation detail with no public API and may change without notice.
      • [x] The second argument argument to a triggered signal that normally holds the sender is null.
      • [x] If a signal is triggered and there's no corresponding receiver it is run a single time without an element passed to the third argument (this is the same as data-s as well).
      • [x] Triggers are sent as events that bubble up the DOM and processed individually by an bitty instance that has a corresponding function (i.e. there is no guarantee that the triggers will occur in the order they are added. Processing things in a given order can be done by triggering a single function at a time with each function calling the next one in the chain when it's done).
      Examples
      • Trigger a signal from inside another function. [0010-single-trigger]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_9FE51_bravo">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_9FE51_alfa">Send 9FE51_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_9FE51_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_9FE51_bravo");
}

export function signal_9FE51_bravo(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0010-single-trigger/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0010-single-trigger/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_9FE51_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_9FE51_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Trigger multiple signals. [0020-multiple-triggers]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_68843_bravo">waiting</div>
<div data-r="signal_68843_charlie">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_68843_alfa">Send 68843_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_68843_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_68843_bravo signal_68843_charlie");
}

export function signal_68843_bravo(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}

export function signal_68843_charlie(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0020-multiple-triggers/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0020-multiple-triggers/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_68843_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_68843_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });

  const checkEls2 = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_68843_charlie]");
  checkEls2.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Triggered signals without a receiver fire once. [0030-no-receiver]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_EC577_charlie">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_EC577_alfa">Send EC577_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_EC577_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_EC577_bravo");
}

export function signal_EC577_bravo(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_EC577_charlie");
}

export function signal_EC577_charlie(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result

        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_EC577_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_EC577_charlie]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The incoming event type is `bittytrigger`. (Note that the internals of the event itself are private implementation details that may change without notice.) [0040-event-type-check]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_94F67_bravo">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_94F67_alfa">Send 94F67_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_94F67_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_94F67_bravo");
}

export function signal_94F67_bravo(ev, __, el) {
  if (ev.type === "bittytrigger") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0040-event-type-check/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0040-event-type-check/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_94F67_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_94F67_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The second argument (i.e. the `sender` argument) is `null` from `.trigger()`. [0050-arg2-is-null]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_3416E_bravo">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_3416E_alfa">Send 3416E_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_3416E_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_3416E_bravo");
}

export function signal_3416E_bravo(_, sender, el) {
  if (sender === null) {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_3416E_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_3416E_bravo]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • The third argument passed to a function from trigger is `null` if there is no corresponding receiver (i.e. element with a `data-r` that has the value of the signal). [0060-arg3-null-if-no-receiver]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_DEF6F_charlie">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_DEF6F_alfa">Send DEF6F_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_DEF6F_alfa(_, __, ___) {
  bitty.trigger("signal_DEF6F_bravo");
}

export function signal_DEF6F_bravo(_, __, el) {
  if (el === null) {
    bitty.trigger("signal_DEF6F_charlie");
  }
}

export function signal_DEF6F_charlie(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/trigger/0060-arg3-null-if-no-receiver/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_DEF6F_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_DEF6F_charlie]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • uuid(dashes = true) [uuid]
      Description
      • [x] Generates a random UUID with self.crypto.randomUUID().
      • [] Passing false for the optional argument removes the dashes from the UUID.
      Examples
      • Get a standard v4 UUID. [0010-uuid-with-dashes]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_B5C78_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_B5C78_alfa">Send B5C78_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_B5C78_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.uuid();
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/uuid/0010-uuid-with-dashes/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/uuid/0010-uuid-with-dashes/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_B5C78_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\w{8}-\w{4}-\w{4}-\w{4}-\w{12}/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_B5C78_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Generate a 32 character string from a UUID with the dashes removed. [0020-uuid-without-dashes]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_26E12_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_26E12_alfa">Send 26E12_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_26E12_alfa(_, __, el) {
  el.innerHTML = bitty.uuid(false);
}

        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/uuid/0020-uuid-without-dashes/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>

        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0030-bitty-functions/uuid/0020-uuid-without-dashes/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_26E12_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\w{32}/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_26E12_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
  • page assets [0035-page-assets]
    • auto loaded page data [page-data]
      Description
      • [] data can be added directly to a page by including a string of JSON in a <script> tag with a type of applicaiton/json and an id attribute.
      • [] The data can be accessed from bitty.data["id"] where id is the value of the id attribute.
      Examples
      • Auto load data from the page. [0010-load-page-data]
        HTML
        <script id="data_signal_A4B07_alfa" type="application/json">
{ "key_signal_A4B07_alfa": "value_signal_A4B07_alfa" }
</script>

<div data-r="signal_A4B07_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A4B07_alfa">Send A4B07_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A4B07_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const checkValue = bitty.data["data_signal_A4B07_alfa"].key_signal_A4B07_alfa;
  if (checkValue === "value_signal_A4B07_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-assets/page-data/0010-load-page-data/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-assets/page-data/0010-load-page-data/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A4B07_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A4B07_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • auto loaded page SVGs [page-svgs]
      Description
      • [x] SVGs can be included on the page by placing them as the first element inside a <template> tag on the page with an id attribute.
      • [x] SVGs loaded this way are available in bitty.svg["id"] where id is the value of the id attribute.
      Examples
      • Auto load SVGs from the page. [0010-load-svgs]
        HTML
        <template id="svg_signal_24CEF_alfa">
  <svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
    <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
    <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">svg</text>
  </svg>
</template>

<div data-r="signal_24CEF_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_24CEF_alfa">Send 24CEF_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_24CEF_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const check = bitty.svg["svg_signal_24CEF_alfa"].querySelector("text");
  if (check.innerHTML === "svg") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-assets/page-svgs/0010-load-svgs/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-assets/page-svgs/0010-load-svgs/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_24CEF_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_24CEF_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
    • auto loaded page templates [page-templates]
      Description
      Examples
      • Use an auto loaded template. [0010-load-page-template]
        HTML
        <template id="template_signal_A6DC9_alfa">
  <div>Content signal_A6DC9_alfa</div>
</template>

<div data-r="signal_A6DC9_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_A6DC9_alfa">Send A6DC9_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_A6DC9_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  const template = bitty.template["template_signal_A6DC9_alfa"];
  const result = template.querySelector("div").innerHTML;
  if (result === "Content signal_A6DC9_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-assets/page-templates/0010-load-page-template/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0035-page-assets/page-templates/0010-load-page-template/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_A6DC9_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_A6DC9_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
  • event functions [0040-event-extras]
    • copy() [copy]
      Description
      • [x] Copies the contents of the .target from the incoming event to the operating system copy/paste board.
      • [x] The .value is copied if it exists.
      • [] Otherwise, the .innerHTML is copied.
      Examples
      • Copy the `.value` of the incoming event's `.target` if it exists. [0010-copy-value]
        HTML
        <button data-s="signal_0F782_alfa" value="Content 0F782">Copy 0F782_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_0F782_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  ev.copy();
}
        Result
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0040-event-extras/copy/0010-copy-value/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-test-status="0"></div>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0040-event-extras/copy/0010-copy-value/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

// This example must be tested manually since
// it deals with the operating system's
// copy/paste system.
      • Copy the contents of the .innerHTML. [0020-copy-inner-html]
        HTML
        <button data-s="signal_D857D_alfa">Copy Content D857D</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_D857D_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  ev.copy();
}
        Result
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0040-event-extras/copy/0020-copy-inner-html/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-test-status="0"></div>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0040-event-extras/copy/0020-copy-inner-html/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

// This example must be tested manually since it
// deals with the copy/paste system of
// the operating system.
    • prop(key) [prop]
      Description
      • [x] Returns the value of a data-KEY attribute as a string if it exist on the target.
      • [x] If there is no data-KEY on the event target the method searches ancestors for the first one with a data-KEY attribute and returns its value.
      • [x] If neither the target element or any of its ancestors have a data-KEY attribute then undefined is returned.
      Examples
      • Get the value from a `data-KEY` attribute as a string. [0010-get-prop-on-target]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_917D0_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_917D0_alfa" data-key="prop_917D0">Send 917D0_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_917D0_alfa(ev, __, el) {
  if (ev.prop("key") === "prop_917D0") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0040-event-extras/prop/0010-get-prop-on-target/test_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        bitty tag for the test verification
        <bitty-8-0 data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/sections/0040-event-extras/prop/0010-get-prop-on-target/test_verification_javascript.js"></bitty-8-0>
        Verification JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export async function runTest() {
  bitty.qs("[data-s~=signal_917D0_alfa]").click();
  await bitty.sleep(200);
  const pattern = /\d\d\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\d/;
  const checkEls = bitty.qsa("[data-r~=signal_917D0_alfa]");
  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;
      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });
}
      • Get the prop from the dataset of an ancestor if the event target doesn't have on itself. [0020-get-prop-from-ancestor]

  checkEls.forEach((checkEl) => {
    if (checkEl.innerHTML === "todo") {
      checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 1;
    } else {
      const match = checkEl.innerHTML.match(pattern);
      if (match !== null) {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 0;

      • [x] If there is no data-KEY on the event target the method searches ancestors for the first one with a data-KEY attribute and returns its value.
      • [x] If neither the target element or any of its ancestors have a data-KEY attribute then undefined is returned.

    • setProp(key, value) [setProp]
      Description
      • [x] Sets or updates the ev.target.dataset.KEY to the specified value.
      Examples
      • TKTKTK: Test name [0010-set-prop]
        HTML
        <div data-r="signal_0067F_alfa">waiting</div>
<button data-s="signal_0067F_alfa">Send 0067F_alfa</button>
        JavaScript
        export const bitty = {};

export function signal_0067F_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  ev.setProp("update", "0067F");
  if (ev.target.dataset.update === "0067F") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
        Result
        waiting
        view test harness
        bitty tag for the example

      } else {
        checkEl.dataset.testStatus = 2;
      }
    }
  });

    • val() [val]
      Description
      • [x] Returns the value from the incoming events .target element.

        <div data-r="signal_B8B31_alfa">waiting</div>

        JavaScript

export function signal_B8B31_alfa(ev, sender, el) {
  if (ev.val() === "Input B8B31_alfa") {
    el.innerHTML = bitty.time();
  }
}
